calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-14-2022 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Michael Crotty and Thomas Daggett, plaintiffs. At the request of plaintiffs, the Court ordered the case dismissed without prejudice. JUDGE: SAMUEL K. FENG; Clerk: J. Rumsey; Proceedings not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-01-2022 Set For Order To Show Cause On Oct-11-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-19-2022 Continued To Aug-10-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-20-2022 Continued To May-11-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Plaintiff'S Ex Parte Application/Emergency Petition For For Injunctive Relief Or Any Other Remedy Available
Off Calendar Oct-13-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Eigil Qwist, Petitioner, appeared on his own behalf.

Hearing is held (as fully reported in the reporter's notes). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Plaintiff'S Ex Parte Application/Emergency Petition For For Injunctive Relief Or Any Other Remedy Available:
After argument, the Court denied Petitioner's emergency petition for injunctive relief or any other lawful remedy in its entirety. The court explained first that Petitioner had not named the City and County of San Francisco as a respondent or given it notice of the ex parte application, and that for the court to grant relief against it on an ex parte basis would violate the California Rules of Court and fundamental principles of due process. The court also found that even if proper notice had been given, Petitioner, and the individuals who filed joinders in the emergency petition, had not met any of the requirements for injunctive relief. Petitioner did not show any likelihood of prevailing on the merits of his claims against the City objecting to its mandatory vaccination policy; did not show that he is likely to suffer irreparable harm in the absence of preliminary relief; that the balance of equities tips in his favor; or that an injunction would be in the public interest.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
